principle: Each retargeting touch addresses the specific alignment threshold that FAILED on the previous touch. Standard retargeting repeats the same message. This system deploys a DIFFERENT mechanism each time, targeting the buyer's weakest alignment dimension.
data_foundation: 3,103 bilateral edges, non-converter sub-clustering, threshold failure analysis with inferential statistics (t-test, p<0.05)

careful_truster.why_they_fail: High negativity_bias_match (fail=0.469 vs conv=0.169) means they weight negative information too heavily. Low brand_trust_fit (fail=0.319 vs conv=0.428) means the brand hasn't earned their trust yet. Low emotional_resonance (fail=0.358 vs conv=0.501) means the messaging felt transactional, not human.

sub_0.label: The Almost-Convinced
sub_0.description: Good processing and appeal scores but low anchor_susceptibility. They evaluated but couldn't justify the price relative to alternatives.
sub_0.creative: Compare LUXY Ride price to hotel car service or Uber Black surge. 'JFK to Manhattan: Uber Black surge at 9 PM = $85-120. LUXY Ride = $65 guaranteed. Always.' Make the value comparison explicit.

sub_1.label: The Fear-Frozen
sub_1.description: Very high negativity_bias — they read the bad reviews and froze. Can't get past the fear of no-show/bad experience.
sub_1.creative: Testimonial from someone who was ALSO skeptical: 'After 2 no-shows with [competitor], I was done with car services. My colleague insisted I try LUXY Ride. Driver was early. Car was clean. I've used them 11 times since.' The key: the testimonial author SHARES their psychology.

sub_2.label: The Uninspired
sub_2.description: Low mental_simulation and appeal resonance. The messaging didn't create a vivid picture of the experience. They couldn't imagine themselves using the service.
sub_2.creative: Specific, sensory-rich scene: 'Tuesday morning. 5:47 AM. Your LUXY Ride driver David is already at your building. Black Mercedes, engine running. Your name on the screen. Coffee-temperature water in the door. You get in, review your deck on the way to JFK. You arrive at Terminal 4 at 6:23 — exactly when you planned.'

sequence.creative_type: Comparative rational — numbers, ratings, guarantee details
sequence.rationale: First touch establishes credibility. appeals_comparative (r=+0.163) and appeals_rational (r=+0.126) convert this archetype.

sequence.trigger: Visited site but did not book within 48h
sequence.creative_type: Deeper evidence — specific review quotes with names, verified ride count, guarantee details
sequence.rationale: brand_trust_fit is the #1 converter (r=+0.619). Touch 2 deepens the trust evidence. NOT warmer — more factual.

sequence.trigger: Returned to site OR 72h after touch 2
sequence.creative_type: Specific customer testimonial — but framed as EVIDENCE, not emotional story. 'Michael R., 47 rides, zero issues.'
sequence.rationale: emotional_resonance is the #2 converter (r=+0.607) but must be deployed as PROOF not warmth. The testimonial creates emotional identification without triggering the anti-sincerity reaction.

sequence.creative_type: Direct price comparison + risk removal. 'Your ride to JFK: $65 guaranteed. No surge. Cancel free 24h before. Book now.'
sequence.rationale: Abandoned booking = price/commitment friction. Address spending_pain directly with guarantee.

sequence.trigger: 14 days after first visit, no conversion
sequence.creative_type: Pure numbers: '3,247 rides. 4.8 stars. Join them.' Minimal copy, maximum evidence.
sequence.rationale: Last attempt uses social_proof (r=+0.148). If 5 touches haven't converted, move to suppression.

suppression_rules: After touch 5 with no conversion: suppress 30 days
If CTR drops below 0.03% after touch 3: suppress immediately
If user visits a competitor site (detected via 3rd party): restart sequence at touch 1 with urgency variant

status_seeker.why_they_fail: Regulatory fit failure (fail=0.036 vs conv=0.091) — the ad wasn't aspirational enough. Brand trust gap (fail=0.429 vs conv=0.471) — they need more status confirmation.

sub_0.label: The Negativity-Blocked
sub_0.description: Low negativity_bias_match — they encountered negative information about luxury car services and it blocked their promotion-focused decision.
sub_0.creative: Override negative with status: 'Fortune 500 executives don't worry about rideshare reviews. They book LUXY Ride. luxyride.com'

sub_1.label: The Trust-Gap
sub_1.description: High negativity but low emotional resonance and low brand trust. They want to believe but haven't seen enough proof.
sub_1.creative: Credential-heavy: '400+ companies. Mercedes S-Class fleet. Chauffeurs with 3+ years experience and background verification. luxyride.com'

sequence.creative_type: Self-expression + social visibility. 'Your arrival is your introduction.'

sequence.trigger: Visited site but did not book within 24h
sequence.creative_type: Stronger aspirational framing. 'Not a ride. A statement. Book your chauffeur.'
sequence.rationale: regulatory_fit_score gap (d=+0.742). Push harder on promotion/gain framing.

sequence.creative_type: Authority proof: fleet specs, company stats, named client testimonial.
sequence.rationale: brand_trust_fit gap (d=+0.510). Status seekers trust credentials, not warmth.

suppression_rules: After touch 3 with no conversion: suppress 14 days
Status seekers have LOW reactance — can re-engage after 14-day cooling

easy_decider.why_they_fail: Processing_route_match failure (fail=0.480 vs conv=0.618, d=+1.364) — the messaging was too complex for their low-deliberation style. Composite alignment also low — overall fit was poor.

sub_0.label: The Motive-Mismatch
sub_0.description: Very low evolutionary_motive_match — the ad spoke to the wrong need entirely.
sub_0.creative: Simple motive-match: 'Airport pickup? LUXY Ride. Done.' or 'Client dinner? Your car is booked. luxyride.com'

sub_1.label: The Over-Processed
sub_1.description: Higher involvement and construal than typical easy deciders — they were asked to think too much.
sub_1.creative: Maximum simplicity: 'LUXY Ride. Tap. Book. Arrive. luxyride.com' — under 10 words total.

sequence.creative_type: Ultra-simple. 'Book in 30 seconds. Arrive in style.'

sequence.trigger: Visited site but did not book within 24h
sequence.creative_type: Reduce to absolute minimum: 'Your ride is waiting. [Book Now button]'
sequence.rationale: processing_route_match is the #1 failure (d=+1.364). The original ad was still too complex.

sequence.creative_type: 'Cancel free anytime. No commitment. Just book.' — address the commitment anxiety.
sequence.rationale: composite_alignment gap (d=+1.318) suggests overall friction. Remove ALL barriers.

suppression_rules: After touch 3: suppress 7 days (they decide fast or not at all)
Easy deciders don't deliberate — if 3 touches don't work, more won't help

cross_archetype_rules.archetype_reclassification: If a user classified as status_seeker on touch 1 shows careful_truster behavior (returns to compare, reads reviews), reclassify and switch to careful_truster sequence.
cross_archetype_rules.booking_abandonment: Any archetype that starts booking and abandons gets a UNIVERSAL touch within 2 hours: 'Your ride is saved. Complete your booking: [link]' — pure CTA, no persuasion.
cross_archetype_rules.competitive_signal: If a user visits LUXY Ride site AND a competitor site within 72h (detected via pixel/3rd party), escalate to comparison creative: 'LUXY Ride vs [competitor]: [differentiator].'
cross_archetype_rules.post_conversion: After conversion, STOP all retargeting. Switch to retention track (easy_decider sequence with recognition language: 'Welcome back').
cross_archetype_rules.frequency_across_sequence: Maximum 1 retargeting impression per 24h. Maximum 5 total in sequence. Reactance recovery: if CTR < 0.03% at any point, pause 72h.

implementation_in_stackadapt.pixel_setup: Install retargeting pixel on: (1) homepage, (2) pricing page, (3) booking start page, (4) booking confirmation page
implementation_in_stackadapt.creative_rotation: Do NOT use StackAdapt's standard creative rotation. Each touch MUST use a DIFFERENT creative mapped to the sequence above. Set up as sequential campaigns with audience exclusion rules.
implementation_in_stackadapt.measurement: Track per-touch conversion rate separately. The hypothesis: each subsequent touch should convert at a HIGHER rate than the previous (because it addresses a more specific failure). If touch N converts lower than touch N-1, the mechanism mapping is wrong for that archetype.
